items.question: Present continuous: The bridge ___ at the moment.
explanations: 'Is repaired' is present simple passive, not continuous.
Correct! Continuous passive = is being + participle: 'is being repaired'.
'Is repairing' is active continuous.
'Has been repaired' is present perfect passive.

items.question: Present perfect: The parcels ___ already.
explanations: 'Have delivered' is active and misses 'been'.
Correct! Present perfect passive = have been + participle: 'have been delivered'.
'Are delivered' is present simple, but 'already' suggests the perfect.
'Were delivered' is past simple, not present perfect.

items.question: Modal: This bottle ___ .
explanations: 'Can recycle' is active; the bottle doesn't recycle itself.
Correct! Modal passive = modal + be + participle: 'can be recycled'.
'Can be recycle' uses the base verb; use 'recycled'.
'Is can recycled' is not correct English.

items.question: Past perfect: By the time we arrived, the room ___ .
explanations: 'Had cleaned' is active; the room was cleaned by someone.
Correct! Past perfect passive = had been + participle: 'had been cleaned'.
'Was cleaning' is active continuous.
'Has been cleaned' is present perfect, but the context is earlier past.

items.question: Past continuous: When I called, my car ___ .
explanations: 'Was repaired' is past simple passive, not continuous.
Correct! Past continuous passive = was being + participle: 'was being repaired'.
'Is being repaired' is present, not past.
'Had been repaired' would mean it was already finished.

items.question: Future: The results ___ next week.
explanations: 'Will announce' is active and needs a doer.
Correct! Future passive = will be + participle: 'will be announced'.
'Will be announce' uses the base verb; use 'announced'.
'Are announced' is present, but 'next week' needs the future.

items.question: Correct this: 'The report must completed by Friday.'
options: The report must be completed by Friday.
The report must completed by Friday.
The report must be complete by Friday.
The report must been completed by Friday.
explanations: Correct! Modal passive = modal + be + participle: 'must be completed'.
This is missing 'be'.
'Complete' is the base/adjective; the participle is 'completed'.
'Must been' is wrong — use 'must be'.

items.question: Present simple: Millions of emails ___ every second.
explanations: 'Send' is active and needs a doer.
Correct! Present passive = are + participle: 'are sent'.
'Are sending' is active continuous.
'Have sent' is active present perfect.

items.question: Present perfect (singular): The decision ___ .
explanations: 'The decision' is singular, so use 'has', not 'have'.
Correct! 'has been made' — present perfect passive, singular subject.
'Has made' is active and misses 'been'.
'Was been made' is not correct English.

items.question: Which passive form is best avoided as too clumsy?
explanations: 'Is being built' is a normal continuous passive.
Correct! 'Will be being built' (future continuous passive) is clumsy — rephrase.
'Has been built' is a normal perfect passive.
'Was built' is a normal past passive.
